title: 10 best herbal essential oils for stomach pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for stomach pain are naturally derived oils extracted from plants, providing relief from digestive discomfort. These oils help ease stomach pain by reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and calming the nervous system. The best herbal essential oils for stomach pain include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum verum, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Cuminum cyminum, Angelica archangelica, and Piper nigrum. If left untreated, stomach pain can disrupt daily life, causing fatigue, loss of appetite, and anxiety, ultimately affecting work, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines. This can provide relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and dyspepsia. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, chamomile essential oil can help alleviate stomach pain and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile essential oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that may be causing stomach pain.
Calming and soothing: Chamomile essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain azulene, a sesquiterpene lactone responsible for the plant's calming effects, which can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers ensuring a minimum of one pint is collected.
Dry the flowers using a single-layer tray or screens for 7-10 days.
Fill a clean glass jar with dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the top third full.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and seal the jar.
Place the jar in a warm dark place for 2 weeks shaking daily before straining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract. The oil's glycyrrhizin compounds also have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to calm inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. Additionally, licorice essential oil has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive system, easing cramps and spasms that can contribute to stomach pain. Overall, it is a natural remedy that can provide quick relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Licorice essential oil helps to relax and calm spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach cramps and pain caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), colitis, and other conditions.

Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice essential oil has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort associated with conditions like gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Soften-and-heal property: Licorice essential oil has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, which can help protect the stomach lining from acid erosion and reduce inflammation associated with conditions like gastritis and ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm digestive issues.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, including ulcers and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and rinse them under cold running water.
Dry the roots in a single layer at room temperature for 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steam-distill the root pieces with 200 milliliters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 1 hour.
Filter the distillate through cheesecloth and store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which effectively soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's compounds, such as fenchol and anethole, work together to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach discomforts. Additionally, fennel oil's carminative properties help to expel gas and relieve bloating, providing rapid relief from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, relieving bloating, cramps, and flatulence by reducing gas production in the gut.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, ulcers, and other inflammatory conditions.
Spasmodic relief properties: The anise-like aroma of fennel essential oil has a relaxing effect on smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, providing relief from cramps, spasms, and colic.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which are believed to help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal disorders, including indigestion, bloating, and cramps, due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using 10 grams of foeniculum vulgare per liter of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, ready for use after 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When inhaled, the oil can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that often accompany stomach discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can ease nausea and indigestion by calming the gut and improving digestion, providing quick relief from abdominal pain. The aroma of peppermint essential oil also has a calming effect on the mind and body, further aiding in stress reduction and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a contributing factor to stomach pain.
Antispasmodic property: The menthol content in peppermint oil has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle lining the digestive tract, helping to ease spasms and cramps that can cause stomach pain.

Carminative property: Peppermint oil's volatile compounds have a natural ability to release gas and reduce bloating, which can help alleviate stomach discomfort and pain caused by indigestion and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile compounds, peppermint roots are used to create essential oils that relieve digestive discomfort and ease nausea.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in menthone and other esters, making them a popular choice for creating essential oils that soothe stomach cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase high-quality dried peppermint.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and transfer them to a clean glass jar with an airtight lid.
Add 20 grams of chopped peppermint leaves to 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its unique blend of compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil's active ingredients, including carvone and limonene, work to soothe the digestive system by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the abdominal area. This can provide effective relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Regular use of caraway essential oil may also help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Caraway essential oil helps to expel gas from the digestive system, relieving bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion and flatulence.
Antispasmodic property: The oil's ability to relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract helps to alleviate cramps and spasms that can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Caraway essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach pain-related disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, caraway roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive discomforts.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a unique compound called cuminaldehyde, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making them useful for alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried seeds of carum carvi and crush them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine the crushed seed powder with carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3 at room temperature.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 24 hours under airtight lid to facilitate oil extraction from seeds.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and squeeze out excess liquid using gentle pressure for 10 minutes.
Transfer the filtered oil into dark glass bottle and store it in cool place protected from light for at least 7 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues such as nausea, cramps, and bloating. The oil's active compounds, like gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, ginger essential oils can help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, making it a popular natural remedy for alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger essential oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, which can relieve nausea, bloating, and cramps associated with stomach pain due to its ability to increase gastric motility.

Anti-Inflammatory Agent: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating conditions such as gastritis and ulcerative colitis that cause stomach pain.

Natural Analgesic: Ginger's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain associated with stomach issues, such as menstrual cramps, acid reflux, and indigestion, by reducing pain transmission to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stomach pain.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the ginger plant is the thick underground stem that contains most of the plant's medicinal properties, including those that help soothe digestive issues and reduce stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots in a single layer at room temperature for three days.
Steam distill the dried ginger roots using a copper still at 100 degrees celsius for four hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from any impurities through centrifugation at 4000 rpm for ten minutes.
Store the final zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. Its warm and comforting aroma relaxes the abdominal muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, allowing for smoother digestion and a sense of relief from stomach distress. By incorporating Ceylon cinnamon essential oils into your daily routine, you can find natural relief from stomach pain and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: This property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Antimicrobial property: Cinnamon essential oil has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which may help combat Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) infections, a common cause of stomach ulcers and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain a high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: The roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant are rich in bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh cinnamomum verum leaves in the morning for optimal oil yield.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using 20 kg of fresh material and 10 liters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator funnel within 30 minutes after distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or sediment before storage in dark glass bottles.
Store the filtered cinnamomum verum essential oil at room temperature for up to 6 months with minimal exposure to light and air.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with stomach pain because of its natural calming and soothing properties. The oil contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and discomfort. Additionally, marjoram's gentle analgesic properties help to ease spasms and tension in the abdominal area, providing quick relief from stomach pain. When inhaled or applied topically, this essential oil can promote relaxation and calmness, further reducing symptoms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax and calm spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation and swelling in the gut, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Digestive stimulant property: Stimulates digestion and improves gut motility, helping to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing stomach pain.

Leaves: The leaves of sweet marjoram plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum majorana leaves in the morning when they are most fragrant.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by gently washing them with distilled water and patting dry with a clean cloth.
Combine 250g of the cleaned leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 48 hours at room temperature to allow the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with stomach pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm digestive issues. The citrus scent of lime oil also has a natural analgesic effect, which can relieve cramps and spasms in the abdominal area. Additionally, lime oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy digestion, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal lime essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of stomach pain such as bloating and cramping.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of herbal lime essential oil help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach pain and discomfort.

Digestive stimulant: Herbal lime essential oil contains compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve constipation, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort by backing up waste in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant contain a high concentration of limonene, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in alleviating stomach pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the lime plant are rich in citral, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia europaea at dawn when they are most fragrant and potent.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Mince 50 grams of dried leaves and place them in a stainless steel distillation apparatus with 1 liter of water.
Distill the mixture under vacuum using a steam distillation setup for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil and store it in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with stomach pain because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to seasonal availability.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using 20 grams of botanicals per liter of distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the extracted sambucus nigra essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for a minimum of 2 weeks before using or storing it long-term.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stomach pain with herbal essential oils is by diluting a few drops of peppermint oil, ginger oil, or fennel oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the blend topically to the abdomen or massage it onto the soles of your feet. This helps to soothe digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation, promoting relaxation and relief from stomach pain.
